     2→B2: PDF Discovery + B3: PDF Resolver & Downloader
     3→
     4→Two-phase pipeline:
     5→  1. Discover intermediate policy page URLs from the Oscar listing page
     6→  2. Resolve each to a real PDF URL (via __NEXT_DATA__) and download
     7→
     8→Based on ChatGPT deep research findings (research/1chatgpt.md).
     9→"""
    10→
    11→from __future__ import annotations
    12→
    13→import json
    14→import os
    15→import re
    16→import time
    17→import random
    18→import logging
    19→from dataclasses import dataclass
    20→from pathlib import Path
    21→from typing import Optional
    22→from urllib.parse import urljoin
    23→
    24→import requests
    25→from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
    26→from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text
    27→from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
    28→
    29→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    30→
    31→# ── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→SOURCE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical"
    34→BASE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com"
    35→PDF_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "data" / "pdfs"
    36→
    37→UA = (
    38→    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
    39→    "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
    40→    "Chrome/[IP-REDACTED] Safari/537.36"
    41→)
    42→
    43→PDF_URL_RE = re.compile(r'((?:https?:)?//assets\.ctfassets\.net/[^\s"\']+)', re.I)
    44→
    45→# ── HTTP Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→
    47→SESSION = requests.Session()
    48→SESSION.headers.update({
    49→    "User-Agent": UA,
    50→    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    51→    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    52→    "Connection": "keep-alive",
    53→})
    54→
    55→# ── Data Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→
    57→
    58→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    59→class PolicyLink:
    60→    title: str
    61→    intermediate_url: str
    62→
    63→
    64→@dataclass
    65→class ResolvedPolicy:
    66→    title: str
    67→    intermediate_url: str
    68→    pdf_url: Optional[str]
    69→    error: Optional[str] = None
    70→
    71→
    72→# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    73→
    74→
    75→def _sleep_polite(base: float = 0.5) -> None:
    76→    time.sleep(base + random.uniform(0.0, 0.35))
    77→
    78→
    79→def _fetch_with_retries(url: str, *, max_retries: int = 3, base_delay: float = 0.5) -> requests.Response:
    80→    last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
    81→    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
    82→        try:
    83→            if attempt > 1:
    84→                time.sleep(base_delay * attempt)
    85→            resp = SESSION.get(url, timeout=30)
    86→            resp.raise_for_status()
    87→            return resp
    88→        except Exception as e:
    89→            last_err = e
    90→            logger.warning("Attempt %d/%d failed for %s: %s", attempt, max_retries, url, e)
    91→    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to fetch {url} after {max_retries} retries") from last_err
    92→
    93→
    94→def _safe_filename(s: str, max_len: int = 140) -> str:
    95→    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\-.()&]", "", s)
    96→    s = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", s).strip("_")
    97→    return (s[:max_len] or "policy")
    98→
    99→
   100→def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
   101→    """Fix protocol-relative URLs (//assets...) to https."""
   102→    if url.startswith("//"):
   103→        return "https:" + url
   104→    return url
   105→
   106→
   107→# ── B2: Discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   108→
   109→
   110→def discover_intermediate_links() -> list[PolicyLink]:
   111→    """Parse the Oscar listing page and find all PDF intermediate links.
   112→
   113→    Strategy:
   114→      - Find <a> elements whose visible text is exactly 'PDF'
   115→      - Use each href as the intermediate URL
   116→      - Derive title from nearest <li> parent text
   117→      - Dedupe by intermediate URL
   118→    """
   119→    logger.info("Discovering policies from %s", SOURCE_URL)
   120→    resp = _fetch_with_retries(SOURCE_URL, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.0)
   121→    soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, "html.parser")
   122→
   123→    results: list[PolicyLink] = []
   124→    seen: set[str] = set()
   125→
   126→    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
   127→        if a.get_text(strip=True) != "PDF":
   128→            continue
   129→
   130→        href = a["href"].strip()
   131→        intermediate_url = urljoin(BASE_URL, href)
   132→
   133→        if intermediate_url in seen:
   134→            continue
   135→        seen.add(intermediate_url)
   136→
   137→        # Title: parent <li> text minus trailing "PDF"
   138→        li = a.find_parent("li")
   139→        if li:
   140→            title = li.get_text(" ", strip=True)
   141→            title = re.sub(r"\s*PDF\s*$", "", title).strip()
   142→        else:
   143→            prev = a.find_previous(string=True)
   144→            title = prev.strip() if prev else intermediate_url
   145→
   146→        results.append(PolicyLink(title=title, intermediate_url=intermediate_url))
   147→
   148→    logger.info("Discovered %d unique intermediate links", len(results))
   149→    return results
   150→
   151→
   152→# ── B3: PDF URL Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   153→
   154→
   155→def extract_pdf_url(intermediate_html: str) -> Optional[str]:
   156→    """Extract the real PDF URL from an intermediate policy page.
   157→
   158→    Preferred: parse __NEXT_DATA__ JSON and search for ctfassets.net URLs.
   159→    Fallback: regex scan raw HTML.
   160→    """
   161→    soup = BeautifulSoup(intermediate_html, "html.parser")
   162→    script = soup.find("script", id="__NEXT_DATA__")
   163→
   164→    # 1) Next.js payload
   165→    if script and script.string:
   166→        try:
   167→            data = json.loads(script.string)
   168→            stack = [data]
   169→            hits: list[str] = []
   170→            while stack:
   171→                obj = stack.pop()
   172→                if isinstance(obj, dict):
   173→                    stack.extend(obj.values())
   174→                elif isinstance(obj, list):
   175→                    stack.extend(obj)
   176→                elif isinstance(obj, str) and "ctfassets.net" in obj:
   177→                    hits.append(obj)
   178→
   179→            pdf_hits = [h for h in hits if h.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   180→            if pdf_hits:
   181→                return _normalize_url(pdf_hits[0])
   182→            if hits:
   183→                return _normalize_url(hits[0])
   184→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   185→            pass
   186→
   187→    # 2) Regex fallback
   188→    matches = PDF_URL_RE.findall(intermediate_html)
   189→    if not matches:
   190→        return None
   191→
   192→    pdf_matches = [m for m in matches if m.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   193→    result = pdf_matches[0] if pdf_matches else matches[0]
   194→    return _normalize_url(result)
   195→
   196→
   197→def resolve_policies(links: list[PolicyLink]) -> list[ResolvedPolicy]:
   198→    """For each intermediate link, fetch the page and extract the real PDF URL."""
   199→    resolved: list[ResolvedPolicy] = []
   200→
   201→    for idx, link in enumerate(links, 1):
   202→        logger.info("[%d/%d] Resolving: %s", idx, len(links), link.title)
   203→        _sleep_polite(0.5)
   204→
   205→        try:
   206→            resp = _fetch_with_retries(link.intermediate_url, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.5)
   207→            pdf_url = extract_pdf_url(resp.text)
   208→
   209→            if not pdf_url:
   210→                logger.warning("  No PDF URL found on %s", link.intermediate_url)
   211→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   212→                    title=link.title,
   213→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   214→                    pdf_url=None,
   215→                    error="No PDF URL found in __NEXT_DATA__ or HTML",
   216→                ))
   217→            else:
   218→                logger.info("  -> PDF: %s", pdf_url[:80])
   219→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   220→                    title=link.title,
   221→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   222→                    pdf_url=pdf_url,
   223→                ))
   224→        except Exception as e:
   225→            logger.error("  !! Failed to resolve %s: %s", link.intermediate_url, e)
   226→            resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   227→                title=link.title,
   228→                intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   229→                pdf_url=None,
   230→                error=str(e),
   231→            ))
   232→
   233→    return resolved
   234→
   235→
   236→# ── B3: PDF Download ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237→
   238→
   239→def download_pdf(pdf_url: str, out_dir: Path, filename_stem: str) -> str:
   240→    """Stream-download a PDF. Returns the saved file path."""
   241→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   242→    out_path = out_dir / f"{_safe_filename(filename_stem)}.pdf"
   243→
   244→    with SESSION.get(pdf_url, timeout=60, stream=True) as r:
   245→        r.raise_for_status()
   246→        ctype = r.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
   247→        if "pdf" not in ctype.lower() and "octet-stream" not in ctype.lower():
   248→            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected Content-Type {ctype} for {pdf_url}")
   249→
   250→        with open(out_path, "wb") as f:
   251→            for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=64 * 1024):
   252→                if chunk:
   253→                    f.write(chunk)
   254→
   255→    if out_path.stat().st_size < 200:
   256→        raise RuntimeError(f"Downloaded file too small ({out_path.stat().st_size}B), likely error page")
   257→
   258→    return str(out_path)
   259→
   260→
   261→# ── Database Integration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   262→
   263→
   264→def _make_engine() -> Engine:
   265→    db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite+pysqlite:///./data/app.db")
   266→    engine = create_engine(
   267→        db_url,
   268→        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if db_url.startswith("sqlite") else {},
   269→        pool_pre_ping=True,
   270→    )
   271→    if db_url.startswith("sqlite"):
   272→        @event.listens_for(engine, "connect")
   273→        def _set_pragmas(dbapi_conn, _):
   274→            cur = dbapi_conn.cursor()
   275→            cur.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;")
   276→            cur.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
   277→            cur.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;")
   278→            cur.close()
   279→    return engine
   280→
   281→
   282→UPSERT_POLICY_SQL = """
   283→INSERT INTO policies (title, pdf_url, source_page_url)
   284→VALUES (:title, :pdf_url, :source_page_url)
   285→ON CONFLICT(pdf_url) DO UPDATE SET
   286→    title = excluded.title,
   287→    source_page_url = excluded.source_page_url
   288→"""
   289→
   290→INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL = """
   291→INSERT INTO downloads (policy_id, stored_location, http_status, error)
   292→VALUES (:policy_id, :stored_location, :http_status, :error)
   293→"""
   294→
   295→GET_POLICY_ID_SQL = """
   296→SELECT id FROM policies WHERE pdf_url = :pdf_url
   297→"""
   298→
   299→
   300→def run_full_pipeline() -> dict:
   301→    """Run the complete B2+B3 pipeline: discover → resolve → download → store.
   302→
   303→    Returns summary stats.
   304→    """
   305→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")
   306→
   307→    from backend.migrate import run_migrations
   308→    run_migrations()
   309→
   310→    engine = _make_engine()
   311→
   312→    # Phase 1: Discover intermediate links
   313→    links = discover_intermediate_links()
   314→
   315→    # Phase 2: Resolve to real PDF URLs
   316→    resolved = resolve_policies(links)
   317→
   318→    # Phase 3: UPSERT policies + download PDFs
   319→    stats = {"discovered": len(links), "resolved": 0, "downloaded": 0, "failed": 0}
   320→
   321→    with engine.begin() as conn:
   322→        for rp in resolved:
   323→            # Use real PDF URL if resolved, otherwise store intermediate_url
   324→            pdf_url = rp.pdf_url or rp.intermediate_url
   325→
   326→            # UPSERT policy
   327→            conn.execute(text(UPSERT_POLICY_SQL), {
   328→                "title": rp.title,
   329→                "pdf_url": pdf_url,
   330→                "source_page_url": SOURCE_URL,
   331→            })
   332→
   333→            # Get the policy ID
   334→            row = conn.execute(text(GET_POLICY_ID_SQL), {"pdf_url": pdf_url}).first()
   335→            policy_id = row[0]
   336→
   337→            if rp.pdf_url is None:
   338→                # No PDF URL resolved — record as failed download
   339→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   340→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   341→                    "stored_location": "",
   342→                    "http_status": None,
   343→                    "error": rp.error or "Could not resolve PDF URL",
   344→                })
   345→                stats["failed"] += 1
   346→                continue
   347→
   348→            stats["resolved"] += 1
   349→
   350→            # Download the PDF
   351→            _sleep_polite(0.5)
   352→            try:
   353→                stored_path = download_pdf(rp.pdf_url, PDF_DIR, rp.title)
   354→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   355→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   356→                    "stored_location": stored_path,
   357→                    "http_status": 200,
   358→                    "error": None,
   359→                })
   360→                stats["downloaded"] += 1
   361→                logger.info("  OK saved: %s", stored_path)
   362→            except Exception as e:
   363→                logger.error("  !! Download failed for %s: %s", rp.title, e)
   364→                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
   365→                    "policy_id": policy_id,
   366→                    "stored_location": "",
   367→                    "http_status": None,
   368→                    "error": str(e),
   369→                })
   370→                stats["failed"] += 1
   371→
   372→    logger.info("Pipeline complete: %s", stats)
   373→    return stats
   374→
   375→
   376→if __name__ == "__main__":
   377→    import sys
   378→    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
   379→
   380→    from dotenv import load_dotenv
   381→    load_dotenv()
   382→
   383→    stats = run_full_pipeline()
   384→    print(f"\nDone! {stats}")
   385→

-- `BEADS.md` — Work unit tracker with status and dependencies
+- `AGENTS.md` — Agent instructions for bd workflow

@@ -63,6 +64,73 @@ Oscar Medical Guidelines scraper + structured criteria tree explorer. The system
 - **Polite scraping**: 0.5s delay + jitter, retry 3x with backoff, browser-like User-Agent
 - **Schema validation**: Pydantic recursive model validation before DB insert
 
+## Issue Tracking — bd (beads)
+
+This project uses **bd** (Steve Yegge's Beads) for ALL task tracking. Backed by Dolt (version-controlled SQL). See AGENTS.md for full agent workflow.
+
+```bash
+bd ready              # Find unblocked work — START HERE every session
+bd show <id>          # View issue details + description
+bd update <id> --claim  # Claim work atomically
+bd close <id> --reason "Done"  # Complete work
+bd list               # List all open issues
+bd graph --all        # Visualize dependency chain
+bd create "title" -d "context" -t feature -p 1  # Create new issue
+```
+
+**Rules:**
+- Do NOT use markdown TODOs, TASKS.md, or competing task files — bd is the single source of truth
+- Always `bd ready` before starting work to find unblocked issues
+- Create discovered issues with `bd create "title" -d "context" --deps discovered-from:<parent-id>`
+- Include bead ID in commit messages: `git commit -m "Add scraper (full-stack-feb-mw1)"`
+- When closing a bead, update bd BEFORE merging to main: `bd close <id> --reason "Completed"`
+- Never use `bd edit` (interactive) — use `bd update <id> --description "text"` instead
+
+## Git Workflow (parallel-safe, beads-integrated)
+
+**Multiple Claude sessions may run simultaneously on different branches.**
+
+### Session Start
+```bash
+cd /home/gian/Projects/full-stack-feb/full-stack-feb
+git checkout main && git pull origin main
+git checkout -b <descriptive-branch-name>   # e.g. scraper, llm-pipeline, ui-tree
+bd ready                                     # Find what to work on
+bd update <id> --claim                       # Claim the bead
+```
+
+### While Working
+- **Commit + push after EVERY meaningful change** (new file, function complete, bug fix)
+- `git add <files> && git commit -m "description (bead-id)" && git push origin <branch>`
+- Aim for a commit+push every 5-10 minutes — unpushed work is LOST if session dies
+
+### Before Merge to Main (MANDATORY)
+```bash
+# TypeScript type-check MUST pass before any merge to main
+npx tsc --noEmit
+```
+If `tsc` fails, fix ALL type errors on your feature branch before proceeding.
+
+### Merge to Main (end of session)
+```bash
+git add -A && git commit -m "final changes (bead-id)" && git push origin <branch>
+bd close <id> --reason "Completed"           # Close the bead FIRST
+git checkout main && git pull origin main     # Get latest
+git checkout <branch>                         # Back to feature branch
+git merge main                               # Merge main INTO branch (resolve conflicts here)
+npx tsc --noEmit                             # Type-check MUST pass
+git checkout main && git merge <branch>       # Fast-forward merge to main
+git push origin main
+git push origin --delete <branch> && git branch -d <branch>
+```
+
+### Critical Rules
+- NEVER `git push --force` to main

---

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE OVERVIEW

### PROJECT STATUS: ~70% COMPLETE

This is a **Medical Guideline PDF Scraper + Structured Criteria Explorer** for Oscar Health. The project is in **active development** with uncommitted changes on the `scraper-pipeline` branch.

---

## ARCHITECTURE

**Tech Stack:**
- **Backend:** FastAPI + SQLAlchemy Core + SQLite (WAL mode)
- **Frontend:** Vite + React 19 + TypeScript + React Router + Tailwind CSS
- **Task Tracking:** bd (Beads by Steve Yegge) — version-controlled task database
- **PDF Processing:** PyMuPDF (fitz) for text extraction, OpenAI GPT-4o for LLM structuring

**Database:** SQLite at `data/app.db` with 3 core tables:
- `policies` (discovered PDFs with metadata)
- `downloads` (download attempts with status/errors)
- `structured_policies` (LLM-generated criteria trees)

---

## PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
/home/gian/Projects/full-stack-feb/full-stack-feb/
├── README.md                    # Project spec
├── CLAUDE.md                    # Implementation guidance
├── BEADS.md                     # Detailed bead descriptions (legacy reference)
├── AGENTS.md                    # Agent workflow instructions
├── oscar.json                   # Target JSON schema (example)
├── oscar.pdf                    # Sample PDF for research
├── RESEARCH_PROMPTS.md          # Deep research prompts
│
├── backend/                     # Python FastAPI
│   ├── main.py                  # API endpoints (305 lines)
│   ├── scraper.py               # B2+B3 PDF discovery & download (384 lines) [UNCOMMITTED]
│   ├── extractor.py             # B5 PDF text extraction (208 lines) [MODIFIED]
│   ├── structurer.py            # B6 LLM structuring (219 lines)
│   ├── validator.py             # B7 JSON schema validation (123 lines)
│   ├── admin.py                 # Admin chat endpoint (36 lines)

│   ├── migrate.py               # DB schema migration (21 lines)
│   ├── requirements.txt          # Python dependencies
│   └── sql/
│       └── sqlite_schema.sql    # DB DDL (3 tables + indexes)
│
├── frontend/                    # Vite + React + TS
│   ├── package.json

│

└── data/
    ├── app.db                   # SQLite (created on first run)
    └── pdfs/                    # Downloaded PDF storage (created on first run)
```

---

## IMPLEMENTATION STATUS

### COMPLETED (FULLY WORKING)

**B1: Project Scaffold** ✅
- FastAPI + Vite/React/TS environment fully set up
- All dev dependencies installed
- Dev servers boot successfully

**B4: Database Layer** ✅
- SQLite with WAL mode, foreign keys enabled, busy_timeout
- 3 tables: `policies`, `downloads`, `structured_policies`
- UPSERT patterns for idempotent reruns
- Auto-migration on FastAPI startup

**B7: JSON Schema Validator** ✅
- Pydantic recursive validation (RuleNode, StructuredPolicy)
- Checks: operator+rules consistency, no operator without rules
- Location: `backend/validator.py`

**B8: API Server** ✅
- 4 endpoints working:
  - `GET /api/policies` — List all with download status + structured flag
  - `GET /api/policies/:id` — Detail view with download metadata
  - `GET /api/policies/:id/tree` — Raw structured JSON
  - `GET /api/stats` — Aggregate counts
- CORS enabled for localhost:5173
- Raw SQL queries with CTEs for efficient joins

**B9: UI — Policy List** ✅
- Table showing title (linked), PDF link, download badge, structured badge
- Search filter by title
- Responsive design, Tailwind styling

**B10: UI — Tree Viewer** ✅
- Custom recursive component (no third-party tree library)
- Expand/collapse per node, default expand depth 0-1
- AND = green badge, OR = orange badge
- Leaf nodes with checkmark icons
- Monospace rule_id labels with connector lines

---

### IN PROGRESS / UNCOMMITTED (70-90% DONE)

**B2: PDF Discovery** 🔶 ~95% DONE
- **File:** `backend/scraper.py` (384 lines, NEW, uncommitted)
- **Status:** Code complete, not yet committed
- **Implementation:**
  - Parses Oscar clinical guidelines listing page
  - Finds all `<a>` tags with visible text "PDF"
  - Derives title from nearest `<li>` parent
  - Dedupes by intermediate URL
  - Returns list of PolicyLink objects
  - Implements polite scraping (0.5s delay + jitter)
  - Has 3x retries with exponential backoff
  - Browser-like User-Agent

**B3: PDF Resolver + Downloader** 🔶 ~95% DONE
- **File:** `backend/scraper.py` (part of same file)
- **Status:** Code complete, not yet committed
- **Implementation:**
  - Fetches each intermediate policy page
  - Parses `<script id="__NEXT_DATA__">` for real PDF URL
  - Recursive JSON walk to find `ctfassets.net` URLs
  - Prefers `.pdf` suffix; accepts extensionless fallback
  - Regex fallback for malformed JSON
  - Downloads PDFs with stream processing (64KB chunks)
  - Sanity checks (Content-Type, file size > 200 bytes)
  - Stores download outcomes (success/failure, http_status, errors)
  - Returns download metadata (stored_location, http_status, error)

**B5: PDF Text Extractor** 🔶 ~95% DONE
- **File:** `backend/extractor.py` (208 lines, modified but complete)
- **Status:** Code complete; uncommitted enhancement pending
- **Implementation:**
  - Uses PyMuPDF (fitz) with `sort=True` for natural reading order
  - Extracts text from all pages preserving numbered lists
  - Strips headers/footers (page numbers, copyright)
  - Adds page markers: `--- Page N ---`
  - Returns ExtractionResult with text + page_count
  - Includes error handling for missing files

**B5.5: Initial-Only Section Extractor** 🔶 ~90% DONE
- **File:** `backend/extractor.py` (new dataclass + function, uncommitted)
- **Status:** Code complete; newly added but not committed
- **Implementation:**
  - `extract_initial_section(full_text) -> SectionResult`
  - Line-by-line state machine (NOT multi-line regex)
  - START patterns: initial, medical necessity criteria, clinical indications
  - END patterns: continuation, reauthorization, repair, billing codes
  - Confidence scoring: 0.95 for explicit "initial", 0.85 for generic "criteria", 0.30 as fallback
  - Returns SectionResult(text, confidence, logic_string) for observability
  - Gemini-inspired enhancements with compiled regex patterns

**B6: LLM Structuring Pipeline** 🔶 ~90% DONE
- **File:** `backend/structurer.py` (219 lines)
- **Status:** Code complete; tested but incomplete semantic retry logic
- **Implementation:**
  - `structure_text(text, title_hint) -> dict` — sends to OpenAI GPT-4o
  - Uses JSON Mode (NOT Structured Outputs)
  - System prompt: ~100 lines with full medical policy analyst instructions
  - User prompt: injects extracted text in `<EXTRACTED_TEXT>` tags
  - temperature=0.1 for deterministic output
  - max_tokens=4000
  - `run_structuring(limit=10)` picks policies with successful downloads
  - Stores: extracted_text, structured_json, llm_model, llm_prompt, validation_error
  - UPSERT pattern with conflict handling
  - **TODO:** Semantic retry (feed validation errors back to LLM, up to 3 attempts) — not yet implemented
  - **TODO:** JSON repair with `fast-json-repair` library — not yet implemented

---

### NOT YET IMPLEMENTED

**Missing Components:**
1. **Semantic Retry Logic** — When B6 validation fails, should re-prompt LLM with error feedback
2. **JSON Repair** — Use `fast-json-repair` library for malformed LLM output
3. **Enhanced B7 Validator** — Gemini suggested:
   - DFS tree traversal for unique rule_id validation
   - Hierarchy prefix validation (children IDs start with parent prefix)
   - Sequential child ID check (1.1, 1.2, 1.3 not 1.1, 1.3)
   - Depth > 5 warning (possible hallucination)
   - Operator consistency check

**Integration Gaps:**
- Pipeline orchestration script (wire together B2 → B3 → B5 → B5.5 → B6 → B7)
- Admin/CLI interface for batch processing
- Error observability dashboard

---

## GIT HISTORY

**Current Branch:** `scraper-pipeline` (active work)
**Main Branch:** `main` (production)

**Recent Commits:**
```

a70add5 Add B5 text extractor and B6 LLM structuring pipeline
7009033 bd: backup 2026-03-04 16:31
3db0fde bd init: initialize beads issue tracking
979d73f Merge branch 'main' into gemini-research
...
```

**Uncommitted Changes:**
```
Modified:
  - backend/extractor.py (enhanced with B5.5 initial-only section extractor)
  - CLAUDE.md (updated with bd workflow + git workflow docs)

Untracked:
  - backend/scraper.py (NEW 384-line file with B2+B3 complete implementation)
```

---

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS

1. **Raw SQL (NOT ORM)** — SQLAlchemy Core with `text()` queries. Gemini recommended for complex CTEs (window functions, row_number for latest download).

2. **PyMuPDF > pdfplumber** — Gemini's analysis: faster, superior Unicode handling (≥, ≤, ™), `sort=True` preserves hierarchy. pdfplumber better for tables/financial docs.

3. **JSON Mode (NOT Structured Outputs)** — OpenAI rejects recursive Pydantic models. JSON Mode with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` works fine.

4. **Polite Scraping** — 0.5s delay + jitter between requests, 3 retries with backoff, browser-like User-Agent. Oscar's robots.txt allows crawling with no Crawl-delay.

5. **bd (Beads) for Task Tracking** — Integrates with git, supports dependency graphs, auto-syncs to JSONL. AGENTS.md documents full workflow.

---

## DATABASE SCHEMA

**policies** (ALL discovered PDFs)
```sql
id (PK)
title
pdf_url (UNIQUE)
source_page_url
discovered_at (TIMESTAMP)
```

**downloads** (ALL download attempts)
```sql
id (PK)
policy_id (FK)
stored_location
downloaded_at (TIMESTAMP)
http_status
error (nullable)
```

**structured_policies** (AT LEAST 10)
```sql
id (PK)
policy_id (FK, UNIQUE)
extracted_text
structured_json (TEXT JSON)
structured_at (TIMESTAMP)
llm_model
llm_prompt
validation_error (nullable)
```

- `policies` (discovered PDFs with metadata)
- `downloads` (download attempts with status/errors)
- `structured_policies` (LLM-generated criteria trees)

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS

1. **Raw SQL (NOT ORM)** — SQLAlchemy Core with `text()` queries. Gemini recommended for complex CTEs (window functions, row_number for latest download).

2. **PyMuPDF > pdfplumber** — Gemini's analysis: faster, superior Unicode handling (≥, ≤, ™), `sort=True` preserves hierarchy. pdfplumber better for tables/financial docs.

3. **JSON Mode (NOT Structured Outputs)** — OpenAI rejects recursive Pydantic models. JSON Mode with `response_format={"type": "json_object"}` works fine.

4. **Polite Scraping** — 0.5s delay + jitter between requests, 3 retries with backoff, browser-like User-Agent. Oscar's robots.txt allows crawling with no Crawl-delay.

5. **bd (Beads) for Task Tracking** — Integrates with git, supports dependency graphs, auto-syncs to JSONL. AGENTS.md documents full workflow.

---

**policies** (ALL discovered PDFs)
```sql

title
pdf_url (UNIQUE)
source_page_url
discovered_at (TIMESTAMP)
```

**downloads** (ALL download attempts)
```sql
id (PK)
policy_id (FK)
stored_location
downloaded_at (TIMESTAMP)
http_status
error (nullable)
```

**structured_policies** (AT LEAST 10)
```sql
id (PK)
policy_id (FK, UNIQUE)
extracted_text
structured_json (TEXT JSON)
structured_at (TIMESTAMP)
llm_model
llm_prompt
validation_error (nullable)
```

## RUNNING THE PROJECT

**Setup:**
```bash
cd /home/gian/Projects/full-stack-feb/full-stack-feb

# Backend
cd backend && pip install -r requirements.txt
cp ../.env.example ../.env  # Add OPENAI_API_KEY
uvicorn backend.main:app --reload --port 8000

# Frontend (in another terminal)
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev
# Opens at http://localhost:5173
```

**Run Pipelines:**
```bash
# B2 + B3: Discovery + Download
python -m backend.scraper

# B5 + B5.5 + B6: Extract + Structure (at least 10 policies)
python -m backend.structurer 10

# Validate oscar.json
python backend/validator.py
```

Based on the README requirements and current codebase state, here's where things stand:

### A) PDF Discovery (ALL) — ~95% coded, **0% tested/run**

### B) PDF Download (ALL) — ~95% coded, **0% tested/run**
- Also in `backend/scraper.py` — resolves intermediate pages, downloads from Contentful CDN
- Retry + rate limiting implemented

### C) Structuring Pipeline (10+) — ~90% coded, **0% tested/run**
- `backend/extractor.py` — PDF text extraction + initial-only section extraction (uncommitted changes)
- `backend/structurer.py` — OpenAI GPT-4o pipeline, but **missing semantic retry** and **JSON repair**
- `backend/validator.py` — Pydantic schema validation (complete)
- **No structured trees exist in the DB yet**

### D) UI — ~95% coded, **untested with real data**
- Policy list page, detail page, tree viewer all built
- Expand/collapse, AND/OR badges, search filter
- API endpoints working

**Code is ~85-90% written, but 0% of the pipeline has actually been executed.** The entire value of the project — discovering PDFs, downloading them, structuring them with the LLM, and viewing them in the UI — hasn't happened yet. There are also uncommitted files sitting in the working tree.

The hardest remaining risk is the **live pipeline execution** — scraping, downloading, and LLM structuring could surface bugs that don't show up in code review alone.

content.content:
1→"""
     2→B2: PDF Discovery + B3: PDF Resolver & Downloader
     3→
     4→Two-phase pipeline:
     5→  1. Discover intermediate policy page URLs from the Oscar listing page
     6→  2. Resolve each to a real PDF URL (via __NEXT_DATA__) and download
     7→
     8→Based on ChatGPT deep research findings (research/1chatgpt.md).
     9→"""
    10→
    11→from __future__ import annotations
    12→
    13→import json
    14→import os
    15→import re
    16→import time
    17→import random
    18→import logging
    19→from dataclasses import dataclass
    20→from pathlib import Path
    21→from typing import Optional
    22→from urllib.parse import urljoin
    23→
    24→import requests
    25→from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
    26→from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text
    27→from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
    28→
    29→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    30→
    31→# ── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→SOURCE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical"
    34→BASE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com"
    35→PDF_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "data" / "pdfs"
    36→
    37→UA = (
    38→    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
    39→    "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
    40→    "Chrome/[IP-REDACTED] Safari/537.36"
    41→)
    42→
    43→PDF_URL_RE = re.compile(r'((?:https?:)?//assets\.ctfassets\.net/[^\s"\']+)', re.I)
    44→
    45→# ── HTTP Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    46→
    47→SESSION = requests.Session()
    48→SESSION.headers.update({
    49→    "User-Agent": UA,
    50→    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    51→    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    52→    "Connection": "keep-alive",
    53→})
    54→
    55→# ── Data Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    56→
    57→
    58→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    59→class PolicyLink:
    60→    title: str
    61→    intermediate_url: str
    62→
    63→
    64→@dataclass
    65→class ResolvedPolicy:
    66→    title: str
    67→    intermediate_url: str
    68→    pdf_url: Optional[str]
    69→    error: Optional[str] = None
    70→
    71→
    72→# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    73→
    74→
    75→def _sleep_polite(base: float = 0.5) -> None:
    76→    time.sleep(base + random.uniform(0.0, 0.35))
    77→
    78→
    79→def _fetch_with_retries(url: str, *, max_retries: int = 3, base_delay: float = 0.5) -> requests.Response:
    80→    last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
    81→    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
    82→        try:
    83→            if attempt > 1:
    84→                time.sleep(base_delay * attempt)
    85→            resp = SESSION.get(url, timeout=30)
    86→            resp.raise_for_status()
    87→            return resp
    88→        except Exception as e:
    89→            last_err = e
    90→            logger.warning("Attempt %d/%d failed for %s: %s", attempt, max_retries, url, e)
    91→    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to fetch {url} after {max_retries} retries") from last_err
    92→
    93→
    94→def _safe_filename(s: str, max_len: int = 140) -> str:
    95→    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\-.()&]", "", s)
    96→    s = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", s).strip("_")
    97→    return (s[:max_len] or "policy")
    98→
    99→
   100→def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
   101→    """Fix protocol-relative URLs (//assets...) to https."""
   102→    if url.startswith("//"):
   103→        return "https:" + url
   104→    return url
   105→
   106→
   107→# ── B2: Discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   108→
   109→
   110→def discover_intermediate_links() -> list[PolicyLink]:
   111→    """Parse the Oscar listing page and find all PDF intermediate links.
   112→
   113→    Strategy:
   114→      - Find <a> elements whose visible text is exactly 'PDF'
   115→      - Use each href as the intermediate URL
   116→      - Derive title from nearest <li> parent text
   117→      - Dedupe by intermediate URL
   118→    """
   119→    logger.info("Discovering policies from %s", SOURCE_URL)
   120→    resp = _fetch_with_retries(SOURCE_URL, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.0)
   121→    soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, "html.parser")
   122→
   123→    results: list[PolicyLink] = []
   124→    seen: set[str] = set()
   125→
   126→    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
   127→        if a.get_text(strip=True) != "PDF":
   128→            continue
   129→
   130→        href = a["href"].strip()
   131→        intermediate_url = urljoin(BASE_URL, href)
   132→
   133→        if intermediate_url in seen:
   134→            continue
   135→        seen.add(intermediate_url)
   136→
   137→        # Title: parent <li> text minus trailing "PDF"
   138→        li = a.find_parent("li")
   139→        if li:
   140→            title = li.get_text(" ", strip=True)
   141→            title = re.sub(r"\s*PDF\s*$", "", title).strip()
   142→        else:
   143→            prev = a.find_previous(string=True)
   144→            title = prev.strip() if prev else intermediate_url
   145→
   146→        results.append(PolicyLink(title=title, intermediate_url=intermediate_url))
   147→
   148→    logger.info("Discovered %d unique intermediate links", len(results))
   149→    return results
   150→
   151→
   152→# ── B3: PDF URL Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   153→
   154→
   155→def extract_pdf_url(intermediate_html: str) -> Optional[str]:
   156→    """Extract the real PDF URL from an intermediate policy page.
   157→
   158→    Preferred: parse __NEXT_DATA__ JSON and search for ctfassets.net URLs.
   159→    Fallback: regex scan raw HTML.
   160→    """
   161→    soup = BeautifulSoup(intermediate_html, "html.parser")
   162→    script = soup.find("script", id="__NEXT_DATA__")
   163→
   164→    # 1) Next.js payload
   165→    if script and script.string:
   166→        try:
   167→            data = json.loads(script.string)
   168→            stack = [data]
   169→            hits: list[str] = []
   170→            while stack:
   171→                obj = stack.pop()
   172→                if isinstance(obj, dict):
   173→                    stack.extend(obj.values())
   174→                elif isinstance(obj, list):
   175→                    stack.extend(obj)
   176→                elif isinstance(obj, str) and "ctfassets.net" in obj:
   177→                    hits.append(obj)
   178→
   179→            pdf_hits = [h for h in hits if h.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   180→            if pdf_hits:
   181→                return _normalize_url(pdf_hits[0])
   182→            if hits:
   183→                return _normalize_url(hits[0])
   184→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   185→            pass
   186→
   187→    # 2) Regex fallback
   188→    matches = PDF_URL_RE.findall(intermediate_html)
   189→    if not matches:
   190→        return None
   191→
   192→    pdf_matches = [m for m in matches if m.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
   193→    result = pdf_matches[0] if pdf_matches else matches[0]
   194→    return _normalize_url(result)
   195→
   196→
   197→def resolve_policies(links: list[PolicyLink]) -> list[ResolvedPolicy]:
   198→    """For each intermediate link, fetch the page and extract the real PDF URL."""
   199→    resolved: list[ResolvedPolicy] = []
   200→
   201→    for idx, link in enumerate(links, 1):
   202→        logger.info("[%d/%d] Resolving: %s", idx, len(links), link.title)
   203→        _sleep_polite(0.5)
   204→
   205→        try:
   206→            resp = _fetch_with_retries(link.intermediate_url, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.5)
   207→            pdf_url = extract_pdf_url(resp.text)
   208→
   209→            if not pdf_url:
   210→                logger.warning("  No PDF URL found on %s", link.intermediate_url)
   211→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   212→                    title=link.title,
   213→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   214→                    pdf_url=None,
   215→                    error="No PDF URL found in __NEXT_DATA__ or HTML",
   216→                ))
   217→            else:
   218→                logger.info("  -> PDF: %s", pdf_url[:80])
   219→                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   220→                    title=link.title,
   221→                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   222→                    pdf_url=pdf_url,
   223→                ))
   224→        except Exception as e:
   225→            logger.error("  !! Failed to resolve %s: %s", link.intermediate_url, e)
   226→            resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
   227→                title=link.title,
   228→                intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
   229→                pdf_url=None,
   230→                error=str(e),
   231→            ))
   232→
   233→    return resolved
   234→
   235→
   236→# ── B3: PDF Download ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   237→
   238→
   239→def download_pdf(pdf_url: str, out_dir: Path, filename_stem: str) -> str:
   240→    """Stream-download a PDF. Returns the saved file path."""
   241→    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   242→    out_path = out_dir / f"{_safe_filename(filename_stem)}.pdf"
   243→
   244→    with SESSION.get(pdf_url, timeout=60, stream=True) as r:
   245→        r.raise_for_status()

"""
B2: PDF Discovery + B3: PDF Resolver & Downloader

Two-phase pipeline:
  1. Discover intermediate policy page URLs from the Oscar listing page
  2. Resolve each to a real PDF URL (via __NEXT_DATA__) and download

Based on ChatGPT deep research findings (research/1chatgpt.md).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
import re
import time
import random
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional
from urllib.parse import urljoin

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text
from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SOURCE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com/clinical-guidelines/medical"
BASE_URL = "https://www.hioscar.com"
PDF_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "data" / "pdfs"

UA = (
    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) "
    "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
    "Chrome/[IP-REDACTED] Safari/537.36"
)

PDF_URL_RE = re.compile(r'((?:https?:)?//assets\.ctfassets\.net/[^\s"\']+)', re.I)

# ── HTTP Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SESSION = requests.Session()
SESSION.headers.update({
    "User-Agent": UA,
    "Accept": "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
    "Accept-Language": "en-US,en;q=0.9",
    "Connection": "keep-alive",
})

# ── Data Types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PolicyLink:
    title: str
    intermediate_url: str


@dataclass
class ResolvedPolicy:
    title: str
    intermediate_url: str
    pdf_url: Optional[str]
    error: Optional[str] = None


# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _sleep_polite(base: float = 0.5) -> None:
    time.sleep(base + random.uniform(0.0, 0.35))


def _fetch_with_retries(url: str, *, max_retries: int = 3, base_delay: float = 0.5) -> requests.Response:
    last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
        try:
            if attempt > 1:
                time.sleep(base_delay * attempt)
            resp = SESSION.get(url, timeout=30)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            return resp
        except Exception as e:
            last_err = e
            logger.warning("Attempt %d/%d failed for %s: %s", attempt, max_retries, url, e)
    raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to fetch {url} after {max_retries} retries") from last_err


def _safe_filename(s: str, max_len: int = 140) -> str:
    s = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\-.()&]", "", s)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", "_", s).strip("_")
    return (s[:max_len] or "policy")


def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
    """Fix protocol-relative URLs (//assets...) to https."""
    if url.startswith("//"):
        return "https:" + url
    return url


# ── B2: Discovery ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def discover_intermediate_links() -> list[PolicyLink]:
    """Parse the Oscar listing page and find all PDF intermediate links.

    Strategy:
      - Find <a> elements whose visible text is exactly 'PDF'
      - Use each href as the intermediate URL
      - Derive title from nearest <li> parent text
      - Dedupe by intermediate URL
    """
    logger.info("Discovering policies from %s", SOURCE_URL)
    resp = _fetch_with_retries(SOURCE_URL, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.0)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, "html.parser")

    results: list[PolicyLink] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()

    for a in soup.find_all("a", href=True):
        if a.get_text(strip=True) != "PDF":
            continue

        href = a["href"].strip()
        intermediate_url = urljoin(BASE_URL, href)

        if intermediate_url in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(intermediate_url)

        # Title: parent <li> text minus trailing "PDF"
        li = a.find_parent("li")
        if li:
            title = li.get_text(" ", strip=True)
            title = re.sub(r"\s*PDF\s*$", "", title).strip()
        else:
            prev = a.find_previous(string=True)
            title = prev.strip() if prev else intermediate_url

        results.append(PolicyLink(title=title, intermediate_url=intermediate_url))

    logger.info("Discovered %d unique intermediate links", len(results))
    return results


# ── B3: PDF URL Resolution ───────────────────────────────────────────────


def extract_pdf_url(intermediate_html: str) -> Optional[str]:
    """Extract the real PDF URL from an intermediate policy page.

    Preferred: parse __NEXT_DATA__ JSON and search for ctfassets.net URLs.
    Fallback: regex scan raw HTML.
    """
    soup = BeautifulSoup(intermediate_html, "html.parser")
    script = soup.find("script", id="__NEXT_DATA__")

    # 1) Next.js payload
    if script and script.string:
        try:
            data = json.loads(script.string)
            stack = [data]
            hits: list[str] = []
            while stack:
                obj = stack.pop()
                if isinstance(obj, dict):
                    stack.extend(obj.values())
                elif isinstance(obj, list):
                    stack.extend(obj)
                elif isinstance(obj, str) and "ctfassets.net" in obj:
                    hits.append(obj)

            pdf_hits = [h for h in hits if h.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
            if pdf_hits:
                return _normalize_url(pdf_hits[0])
            if hits:
                return _normalize_url(hits[0])
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass

    # 2) Regex fallback
    matches = PDF_URL_RE.findall(intermediate_html)
    if not matches:
        return None

    pdf_matches = [m for m in matches if m.lower().endswith(".pdf")]
    result = pdf_matches[0] if pdf_matches else matches[0]
    return _normalize_url(result)


def resolve_policies(links: list[PolicyLink]) -> list[ResolvedPolicy]:
    """For each intermediate link, fetch the page and extract the real PDF URL."""
    resolved: list[ResolvedPolicy] = []

    for idx, link in enumerate(links, 1):
        logger.info("[%d/%d] Resolving: %s", idx, len(links), link.title)
        _sleep_polite(0.5)

        try:
            resp = _fetch_with_retries(link.intermediate_url, max_retries=3, base_delay=0.5)
            pdf_url = extract_pdf_url(resp.text)

            if not pdf_url:
                logger.warning("  No PDF URL found on %s", link.intermediate_url)
                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
                    title=link.title,
                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
                    pdf_url=None,
                    error="No PDF URL found in __NEXT_DATA__ or HTML",
                ))
            else:
                logger.info("  -> PDF: %s", pdf_url[:80])
                resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
                    title=link.title,
                    intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
                    pdf_url=pdf_url,
                ))
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error("  !! Failed to resolve %s: %s", link.intermediate_url, e)
            resolved.append(ResolvedPolicy(
                title=link.title,
                intermediate_url=link.intermediate_url,
                pdf_url=None,
                error=str(e),
            ))

    return resolved


# ── B3: PDF Download ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def download_pdf(pdf_url: str, out_dir: Path, filename_stem: str) -> str:
    """Stream-download a PDF. Returns the saved file path."""
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out_path = out_dir / f"{_safe_filename(filename_stem)}.pdf"

    with SESSION.get(pdf_url, timeout=60, stream=True) as r:
        r.raise_for_status()
        ctype = r.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
        if "pdf" not in ctype.lower() and "octet-stream" not in ctype.lower():
            raise RuntimeError(f"Unexpected Content-Type {ctype} for {pdf_url}")

        with open(out_path, "wb") as f:
            for chunk in r.iter_content(chunk_size=64 * 1024):
                if chunk:
                    f.write(chunk)

    if out_path.stat().st_size < 200:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Downloaded file too small ({out_path.stat().st_size}B), likely error page")

    return str(out_path)


# ── Database Integration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _make_engine() -> Engine:
    db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite+pysqlite:///./data/app.db")
    engine = create_engine(
        db_url,
        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if db_url.startswith("sqlite") else {},
        pool_pre_ping=True,
    )
    if db_url.startswith("sqlite"):
        @event.listens_for(engine, "connect")
        def _set_pragmas(dbapi_conn, _):
            cur = dbapi_conn.cursor()
            cur.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;")
            cur.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
            cur.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;")
            cur.close()
    return engine


UPSERT_POLICY_SQL = """
INSERT INTO policies (title, pdf_url, source_page_url)
VALUES (:title, :pdf_url, :source_page_url)
ON CONFLICT(pdf_url) DO UPDATE SET
    title = excluded.title,
    source_page_url = excluded.source_page_url
"""

INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL = """
INSERT INTO downloads (policy_id, stored_location, http_status, error)
VALUES (:policy_id, :stored_location, :http_status, :error)
"""

GET_POLICY_ID_SQL = """
SELECT id FROM policies WHERE pdf_url = :pdf_url
"""


def run_full_pipeline() -> dict:
    """Run the complete B2+B3 pipeline: discover → resolve → download → store.

    Returns summary stats.
    """
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")

    from backend.migrate import run_migrations
    run_migrations()

    engine = _make_engine()

    # Phase 1: Discover intermediate links
    links = discover_intermediate_links()

    # Phase 2: Resolve to real PDF URLs
    resolved = resolve_policies(links)

    # Phase 3: UPSERT policies + download PDFs
    stats = {"discovered": len(links), "resolved": 0, "downloaded": 0, "failed": 0}

    with engine.begin() as conn:
        for rp in resolved:
            # Use real PDF URL if resolved, otherwise store intermediate_url
            pdf_url = rp.pdf_url or rp.intermediate_url

            # UPSERT policy
            conn.execute(text(UPSERT_POLICY_SQL), {
                "title": rp.title,
                "pdf_url": pdf_url,
                "source_page_url": SOURCE_URL,
            })

            # Get the policy ID
            row = conn.execute(text(GET_POLICY_ID_SQL), {"pdf_url": pdf_url}).first()
            policy_id = row[0]

            if rp.pdf_url is None:
                # No PDF URL resolved — record as failed download
                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
                    "policy_id": policy_id,
                    "stored_location": "",
                    "http_status": None,
                    "error": rp.error or "Could not resolve PDF URL",
                })
                stats["failed"] += 1
                continue

            stats["resolved"] += 1

            # Download the PDF
            _sleep_polite(0.5)
            try:
                stored_path = download_pdf(rp.pdf_url, PDF_DIR, rp.title)
                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
                    "policy_id": policy_id,
                    "stored_location": stored_path,
                    "http_status": 200,
                    "error": None,
                })
                stats["downloaded"] += 1
                logger.info("  OK saved: %s", stored_path)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error("  !! Download failed for %s: %s", rp.title, e)
                conn.execute(text(INSERT_DOWNLOAD_SQL), {
                    "policy_id": policy_id,
                    "stored_location": "",
                    "http_status": None,
                    "error": str(e),
                })
                stats["failed"] += 1

    logger.info("Pipeline complete: %s", stats)
    return stats


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))

    from dotenv import load_dotenv
    load_dotenv()

    stats = run_full_pipeline()
    print(f"\nDone! {stats}")

1→"""
     2→B6: LLM Structuring Pipeline
     3→
     4→Sends extracted PDF text to OpenAI GPT-4o and returns a structured JSON
     5→criteria tree matching the oscar.json format.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import json
    11→import logging
    12→import os
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→from openai import OpenAI
    17→from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text as sql_text
    18→
    19→from backend.extractor import ExtractionResult, SectionResult, extract_text, extract_initial_section
    20→from backend.validator import validate_structured_json
    21→
    22→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    23→
    24→OSCAR_JSON = json.loads((Path(__file__).parent.parent / "oscar.json").read_text())
    25→
    26→SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a medical policy criteria structurer. You convert medical guideline text into structured JSON decision trees.
    27→
    28→Your output MUST be valid JSON matching this exact schema:
    29→{
    30→  "title": "string - descriptive title of the criteria",
    31→  "insurance_name": "Oscar Health",
    32→  "rules": {
    33→    "rule_id": "string - hierarchical ID like 1, 1.1, 1.1.1",
    34→    "rule_text": "string - the criterion text",
    35→    "operator": "AND or OR - REQUIRED if this node has children",
    36→    "rules": [array of child nodes with same shape - REQUIRED if operator is present]
    37→  }
    38→}
    39→
    40→Rules:
    41→- The root "rules" object is a single node (not an array)
    42→- Leaf nodes have ONLY rule_id and rule_text (no operator, no rules array)
    43→- Non-leaf nodes MUST have both "operator" and "rules"
    44→- operator is "AND" when ALL criteria must be met (phrases like "all of the following", "and")
    45→- operator is "OR" when ANY criterion suffices (phrases like "one of the following", "or")
    46→- Use hierarchical rule_id numbering: 1, 1.1, 1.2, 1.2.1, etc.
    47→- Extract ONLY the INITIAL authorization/approval criteria, NOT reauthorization/continuation
    48→- Keep rule_text concise but complete
    49→
    50→Here is an example of correct output:
    51→""" + json.dumps(OSCAR_JSON, indent=2)
    52→
    53→USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract the INITIAL medical necessity criteria from this Oscar Health clinical guideline and structure them as a JSON decision tree.
    54→
    55→IMPORTANT:
    56→- Extract ONLY the initial criteria (NOT reauthorization, continuation, repair, or revision criteria)
    57→- If there are multiple criteria sections, extract only the first/initial one
    58→- Preserve the AND/OR logic exactly as stated in the document
    59→
    60→Guideline text:
    61→---
    62→{text}
    63→---
    64→
    65→Return ONLY the JSON object, no markdown formatting, no explanation."""
    66→
    67→
    68→def _parse_and_repair(raw: str) -> dict:
    69→    """Parse LLM output, repairing malformed JSON if needed."""
    70→    try:
    71→        return json.loads(raw)
    72→    except json.JSONDecodeError:
    73→        # Strip markdown fencing
    74→        cleaned = raw.strip()
    75→        if cleaned.startswith("```json"):
    76→            cleaned = cleaned[7:]
    77→        if cleaned.startswith("```"):
    78→            cleaned = cleaned[3:]
    79→        if cleaned.endswith("```"):
    80→            cleaned = cleaned[:-3]
    81→        cleaned = cleaned.strip()
    82→
    83→        # Try again after stripping
    84→        try:
    85→            return json.loads(cleaned)
    86→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
    87→            pass
    88→
    89→        # Try fast-json-repair if available
    90→        try:
    91→            from fast_json_repair import repair_json
    92→            repaired = repair_json(cleaned)
    93→            return json.loads(repaired)
    94→        except (ImportError, Exception):
    95→            pass
    96→
    97→        # Try json_repair as fallback
    98→        try:
    99→            import json_repair
   100→            return json_repair.loads(cleaned)
   101→        except (ImportError, Exception):
   102→            pass
   103→
   104→        raise ValueError(f"Irreparable JSON: {raw[:200]}...")
   105→
   106→
   107→def _clean_leaf_nodes(node: dict) -> None:
   108→    """Remove hallucinated operators from leaf nodes."""
   109→    if "rules" not in node or not node.get("rules"):
   110→        node.pop("operator", None)
   111→        node.pop("rules", None)
   112→    else:
   113→        for child in node["rules"]:
   114→            _clean_leaf_nodes(child)
   115→
   116→
   117→def structure_text(text: str, title_hint: str = "", max_attempts: int = 3) -> dict:
   118→    """Send text to OpenAI and get structured JSON back.
   119→
   120→    Includes semantic retry: on validation failure, feeds error back to LLM.
   121→    """
   122→    client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))
   123→
   124→    user_prompt = USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(text=text[:12000])  # Token limit safety
   125→
   126→    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
   127→        response = client.chat.completions.create(
   128→            model="gpt-4o",
   129→            messages=[
   130→                {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
   131→                {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt},
   132→            ],
   133→            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
   134→            temperature=0.1,
   135→            max_tokens=4096,
   136→        )
   137→
   138→        content = response.choices[0].message.content
   139→        result = _parse_and_repair(content)
   140→
   141→        # Clean leaf nodes
   142→        if "rules" in result:
   143→            _clean_leaf_nodes(result["rules"])
   144→
   145→        # Validate
   146→        is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(result)
   147→        if is_valid and (error is None or error.startswith("Warnings:")):
   148→            logger.info("LLM returned valid JSON (attempt %d): %s", attempt + 1, result.get("title", "?"))
   149→            return result
   150→
   151→        # Semantic retry: feed error back

"""
B6: LLM Structuring Pipeline

Sends extracted PDF text to OpenAI GPT-4o and returns a structured JSON
criteria tree matching the oscar.json format.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import logging
import os
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Optional

from openai import OpenAI
from sqlalchemy import create_engine, event, text as sql_text

from backend.extractor import ExtractionResult, SectionResult, extract_text, extract_initial_section
from backend.validator import validate_structured_json

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

OSCAR_JSON = json.loads((Path(__file__).parent.parent / "oscar.json").read_text())

SYSTEM_PROMPT = """You are a medical policy criteria structurer. You convert medical guideline text into structured JSON decision trees.

Your output MUST be valid JSON matching this exact schema:
{
  "title": "string - descriptive title of the criteria",
  "insurance_name": "Oscar Health",
  "rules": {
    "rule_id": "string - hierarchical ID like 1, 1.1, 1.1.1",
    "rule_text": "string - the criterion text",
    "operator": "AND or OR - REQUIRED if this node has children",
    "rules": [array of child nodes with same shape - REQUIRED if operator is present]
  }
}

Rules:
- The root "rules" object is a single node (not an array)
- Leaf nodes have ONLY rule_id and rule_text (no operator, no rules array)
- Non-leaf nodes MUST have both "operator" and "rules"
- operator is "AND" when ALL criteria must be met (phrases like "all of the following", "and")
- operator is "OR" when ANY criterion suffices (phrases like "one of the following", "or")
- Use hierarchical rule_id numbering: 1, 1.1, 1.2, 1.2.1, etc.
- Extract ONLY the INITIAL authorization/approval criteria, NOT reauthorization/continuation
- Keep rule_text concise but complete

Here is an example of correct output:
""" + json.dumps(OSCAR_JSON, indent=2)

USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE = """Extract the INITIAL medical necessity criteria from this Oscar Health clinical guideline and structure them as a JSON decision tree.

IMPORTANT:
- Extract ONLY the initial criteria (NOT reauthorization, continuation, repair, or revision criteria)
- If there are multiple criteria sections, extract only the first/initial one
- Preserve the AND/OR logic exactly as stated in the document

Guideline text:
---
{text}
---

Return ONLY the JSON object, no markdown formatting, no explanation."""


def _parse_and_repair(raw: str) -> dict:
    """Parse LLM output, repairing malformed JSON if needed."""
    try:
        return json.loads(raw)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        # Strip markdown fencing
        cleaned = raw.strip()
        if cleaned.startswith("```json"):
            cleaned = cleaned[7:]
        if cleaned.startswith("```"):
            cleaned = cleaned[3:]
        if cleaned.endswith("```"):
            cleaned = cleaned[:-3]
        cleaned = cleaned.strip()

        # Try again after stripping
        try:
            return json.loads(cleaned)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            pass

        # Try fast-json-repair if available
        try:
            from fast_json_repair import repair_json
            repaired = repair_json(cleaned)
            return json.loads(repaired)
        except (ImportError, Exception):
            pass

        # Try json_repair as fallback
        try:
            import json_repair
            return json_repair.loads(cleaned)
        except (ImportError, Exception):
            pass

        raise ValueError(f"Irreparable JSON: {raw[:200]}...")


def _clean_leaf_nodes(node: dict) -> None:
    """Remove hallucinated operators from leaf nodes."""
    if "rules" not in node or not node.get("rules"):
        node.pop("operator", None)
        node.pop("rules", None)
    else:
        for child in node["rules"]:
            _clean_leaf_nodes(child)


def structure_text(text: str, title_hint: str = "", max_attempts: int = 3) -> dict:
    """Send text to OpenAI and get structured JSON back.

    Includes semantic retry: on validation failure, feeds error back to LLM.
    """
    client = OpenAI(api_key=os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"))

    user_prompt = USER_PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(text=text[:12000])  # Token limit safety

    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
        response = client.chat.completions.create(
            model="gpt-4o",
            messages=[
                {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
                {"role": "user", "content": user_prompt},
            ],
            response_format={"type": "json_object"},
            temperature=0.1,
            max_tokens=4096,
        )

        content = response.choices[0].message.content
        result = _parse_and_repair(content)

        # Clean leaf nodes
        if "rules" in result:
            _clean_leaf_nodes(result["rules"])

        # Validate
        is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(result)
        if is_valid and (error is None or error.startswith("Warnings:")):
            logger.info("LLM returned valid JSON (attempt %d): %s", attempt + 1, result.get("title", "?"))
            return result

        # Semantic retry: feed error back
        logger.warning("Attempt %d validation failed: %s", attempt + 1, error)
        if "continuation" in (error or "").lower():
            user_prompt += "\n\nCRITICAL ERROR: You extracted Continuation criteria. ONLY extract the INITIAL criteria."
        else:
            user_prompt += f"\n\nERROR IN PREVIOUS OUTPUT: {error}\nPlease fix the JSON structure."

    # Return last result even if imperfect
    logger.warning("All %d attempts exhausted, returning last result", max_attempts)
    return result


def _make_engine():
    db_url = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite+pysqlite:///./data/app.db")
    engine = create_engine(
        db_url,
        connect_args={"check_same_thread": False} if db_url.startswith("sqlite") else {},
        pool_pre_ping=True,
    )
    if db_url.startswith("sqlite"):
        @event.listens_for(engine, "connect")
        def _pragmas(dbapi_conn, _):
            cur = dbapi_conn.cursor()
            cur.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;")
            cur.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
            cur.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000;")
            cur.close()
    return engine


UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL = """
INSERT INTO structured_policies (policy_id, extracted_text, structured_json, llm_model, llm_prompt, validation_error)
VALUES (:policy_id, :extracted_text, :structured_json, :llm_model, :llm_prompt, :validation_error)
ON CONFLICT(policy_id) DO UPDATE SET
    extracted_text = excluded.extracted_text,
    structured_json = excluded.structured_json,
    structured_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    llm_model = excluded.llm_model,
    llm_prompt = excluded.llm_prompt,
    validation_error = excluded.validation_error
"""


def run_structuring(limit: int = 10) -> dict:
    """Structure at least `limit` downloaded policies.

    Picks policies that have successful downloads but no structured_policies entry yet.
    """
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(message)s")

    from backend.migrate import run_migrations
    run_migrations()

    engine = _make_engine()

    # Find policies with successful downloads but no structuring yet
    with engine.begin() as conn:
        rows = conn.execute(sql_text("""
            SELECT p.id, p.title, d.stored_location
            FROM policies p
            JOIN downloads d ON d.policy_id = p.id AND d.error IS NULL AND d.stored_location != ''
            LEFT JOIN structured_policies sp ON sp.policy_id = p.id
            WHERE sp.id IS NULL
            ORDER BY p.id
            LIMIT :limit
        """), {"limit": limit}).mappings().all()

    if not rows:
        logger.info("No policies to structure (all done or no downloads)")
        return {"total": 0, "success": 0, "failed": 0}

    stats = {"total": len(rows), "success": 0, "failed": 0}

    for row in rows:
        policy_id = row["id"]
        title = row["title"]
        pdf_path = row["stored_location"]

        logger.info("Structuring: %s (id=%d)", title, policy_id)

        try:
            # Extract text
            extraction = extract_text(pdf_path)
            if extraction.error:
                raise RuntimeError(f"Text extraction failed: {extraction.error}")
            section = extract_initial_section(extraction.text)
            initial_text = section.text

            # Structure with LLM
            result = structure_text(initial_text, title_hint=title)

            # Validate
            is_valid, error = validate_structured_json(result)
            validation_error = error if not is_valid else None

            if validation_error:
                logger.warning("Validation issue for %s: %s", title, validation_error)

            # Store
            with engine.begin() as conn:
                conn.execute(sql_text(UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL), {
                    "policy_id": policy_id,
                    "extracted_text": initial_text[:50000],  # Truncate for storage
                    "structured_json": json.dumps(result),
                    "llm_model": "gpt-4o",
                    "llm_prompt": "v1-system+user-initial-only",
                    "validation_error": validation_error,
                })

            stats["success"] += 1
            logger.info("  OK: %s", title)

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error("  FAILED: %s — %s", title, e)
            # Store the failure
            try:
                with engine.begin() as conn:
                    conn.execute(sql_text(UPSERT_STRUCTURED_SQL), {
                        "policy_id": policy_id,
                        "extracted_text": "",
                        "structured_json": "{}",
                        "llm_model": "gpt-4o",
                        "llm_prompt": "v1-system+user-initial-only",
                        "validation_error": str(e),
                    })
            except Exception:
                pass
            stats["failed"] += 1

    logger.info("Structuring complete: %s", stats)
    return stats


if __name__ == "__main__":
    from dotenv import load_dotenv
    load_dotenv()

    import sys
    limit = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 10
    stats = run_structuring(limit=limit)
    print(f"\nDone! {stats}")

1→"""
     2→B5: PDF Text Extractor
     3→
     4→Extracts text from downloaded PDFs using PyMuPDF (fitz).
     5→Preserves page structure and numbering for LLM consumption.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→from __future__ import annotations
     9→
    10→import logging
    11→import re
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass
    13→from pathlib import Path
    14→from typing import Optional
    15→
    16→import fitz  # PyMuPDF
    17→
    18→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    19→
    20→
    21→@dataclass
    22→class ExtractionResult:
    23→    text: str
    24→    page_count: int
    25→    error: Optional[str] = None
    26→
    27→
    28→def extract_text(pdf_path: str | Path) -> ExtractionResult:
    29→    """Extract text from a PDF file.
    30→
    31→    Returns full text with page markers, preserving numbered lists and hierarchy.
    32→    """
    33→    pdf_path = Path(pdf_path)
    34→    if not pdf_path.exists():
    35→        return ExtractionResult(text="", page_count=0, error=f"File not found: {pdf_path}")
    36→
    37→    try:
    38→        doc = fitz.open(str(pdf_path))
    39→        pages: list[str] = []
    40→
    41→        for page_num in range(len(doc)):
    42→            page = doc[page_num]
    43→            page_text = page.get_text("text", sort=True)
    44→
    45→            # Strip common headers/footers (page numbers, copyright lines)
    46→            lines = page_text.split("\n")
    47→            filtered = _strip_headers_footers(lines, page_num + 1, len(doc))
    48→            pages.append("\n".join(filtered))
    49→
    50→        doc.close()
    51→
    52→        full_text = "\n\n".join(
    53→            f"--- Page {i+1} ---\n{p}" for i, p in enumerate(pages) if p.strip()
    54→        )
    55→
    56→        return ExtractionResult(text=full_text, page_count=len(pages))
    57→
    58→    except Exception as e:
    59→        logger.error("Failed to extract text from %s: %s", pdf_path, e)
    60→        return ExtractionResult(text="", page_count=0, error=str(e))
    61→
    62→
    63→def _strip_headers_footers(lines: list[str], page_num: int, total_pages: int) -> list[str]:
    64→    """Remove common header/footer patterns from extracted lines."""
    65→    filtered = []
    66→    for line in lines:
    67→        stripped = line.strip()
    68→
    69→        # Skip empty lines at start/end (will be re-added by join)
    70→        if not stripped:
    71→            filtered.append("")
    72→            continue
    73→
    74→        # Skip standalone page numbers
    75→        if re.match(r"^\d+$", stripped):
    76→            continue
    77→        if re.match(r"^Page\s+\d+\s*(of\s+\d+)?$", stripped, re.I):
    78→            continue
    79→
    80→        # Skip "Confidential" / copyright footers
    81→        if re.match(r"^(confidential|©|\u00a9|all rights reserved)", stripped, re.I):
    82→            continue
    83→
    84→        filtered.append(line)
    85→
    86→    return filtered
    87→
    88→
    89→@dataclass
    90→class SectionResult:
    91→    text: str
    92→    confidence: float
    93→    logic: str
    94→
    95→
    96→# Patterns indicating the start of the initial medical necessity criteria
    97→_START_PATTERNS = [
    98→    re.compile(r"(?i)initial\s+(criteria|authorization|approval)"),
    99→    re.compile(r"(?i)criteria\s+for\s+medically\s+necessary"),
   100→    re.compile(r"(?i)medical\s+necessity\s+criteria"),
   101→    re.compile(r"(?i)conditions\s+for\s+coverage"),
   102→    re.compile(r"(?i)clinical\s+indications"),
   103→]
   104→
   105→# Patterns indicating the start of a non-initial section (stopping points)
   106→_END_PATTERNS = [
   107→    re.compile(r"(?i)continuation\s+(criteria|therapy|treatment|of\s+therapy)"),
   108→    re.compile(r"(?i)re-?authorization\s+criteria"),
   109→    re.compile(r"(?i)renewal\s+criteria"),
   110→    re.compile(r"(?i)repair[/,]\s*revision"),
   111→    re.compile(r"(?i)conversion\s+criteria"),
   112→    re.compile(r"(?i)removal\s+criteria"),
   113→    re.compile(r"(?i)experimental\s*[/&]\s*investigational"),
   114→    re.compile(r"(?i)relative\s+contraindications"),
   115→    re.compile(r"(?i)applicable\s+billing\s+codes"),
   116→    re.compile(r"(?i)HCPCS\s+(&|and)\s+CPT\s+codes"),
   117→    re.compile(r"(?i)procedures?\s*(&|and)\s*length\s+of\s+stay"),
   118→    re.compile(r"(?i)Medical Necessity Criteria for Reauthorization"),
   119→]
   120→
   121→
   122→def extract_initial_section(full_text: str) -> SectionResult:
   123→    """Extract only the 'Initial' criteria section from full PDF text.
   124→
   125→    Uses a line-by-line state machine approach with confidence scoring.
   126→    Returns SectionResult with text, confidence (0.0-1.0), and extraction logic.
   127→    """
   128→    lines = full_text.split("\n")
   129→    start_idx = -1
   130→    end_idx = -1
   131→    confidence = 0.0
   132→    logic = "No boundaries detected."
   133→    in_criteria = False
   134→
   135→    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
   136→        clean = line.strip()
   137→        if not clean:
   138→            continue
   139→
   140→        if not in_criteria:
   141→            for pattern in _START_PATTERNS:
   142→                if pattern.search(clean):
   143→                    start_idx = i
   144→                    in_criteria = True
   145→                    if "initial" in clean.lower():
   146→                        confidence = 0.95
   147→                        logic = f"Found explicit 'Initial' start at line {i}."
   148→                    else:
   149→                        confidence = 0.85
   150→                        logic = f"Found generic criteria start at line {i}."
   151→                    break
   152→        else:
   153→            for pattern in _END_PATTERNS:
   154→                if pattern.search(clean):
   155→                    end_idx = i
   156→                    logic += f" End marker '{clean[:40]}...' at line {i}."
   157→                    break
   158→
   159→            # Heuristic: uppercase block as section break
   160→            if end_idx == -1 and clean.isupper() and len(clean) > 10:
   161→                if any(kw in clean for kw in ("BACKGROUND", "SUMMARY", "REFERENCES")):
   162→                    end_idx = i
   163→                    logic += f" Uppercase block end at line {i}."
   164→
   165→            if end_idx != -1:
   166→                break
   167→
   168→    if start_idx == -1:
   169→        return SectionResult(
   170→            text=full_text,
   171→            confidence=0.30,
   172→            logic="Fallback: No start boundary found. Returning full text.",
   173→        )
   174→
   175→    if end_idx == -1:
   176→        end_idx = len(lines)
   177→        confidence -= 0.15
   178→        logic += " Reached EOF without explicit end marker."
   179→
   180→    section = "\n".join(lines[start_idx:end_idx])
   181→    logger.info(
   182→        "Extracted initial section: %d chars (confidence=%.2f)", len(section), confidence
   183→    )
   184→    return SectionResult(text=section, confidence=confidence, logic=logic)
   185→
   186→
   187→if __name__ == "__main__":
   188→    import sys
   189→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
   190→
   191→    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
   192→        print("Usage: python -m backend.extractor <pdf_path>")
   193→        sys.exit(1)
   194→
   195→    result = extract_text(sys.argv[1])
   196→    print(f"Pages: {result.page_count}")
   197→    print(f"Text length: {len(result.text)} chars")
   198→    if result.error:
   199→        print(f"Error: {result.error}")
   200→    else:
   201→        # Show first 500 chars
   202→        print(result.text[:500])
   203→        print("---")
   204→        # Try initial section extraction
   205→        section = extract_initial_section(result.text)
   206→        print(f"\nInitial section: {len(section.text)} chars (confidence={section.confidence:.2f})")
   207→        print(f"Logic: {section.logic}")
   208→        print(section.text[:500])
   209→
